skill.name: manager-planner
skill.description: Coordinates Pukaist evidence-review agents, requires plan approval before action, runs system checks, and audits outputs for citation quality.
skill.summary: Plan and supervise multi-agent evidence review workflows with integrity checks.
skill.author: ACSKamloops

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution, and those are mostly false positives. Human review confirmed that the skill does instruct agents to run repository-local Python utilities and cleanup commands, so it carries a legitimate external-command risk but no evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Local Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run local Python utilities for tests, health checks, cleanup, and queue reconciliation. This is aligned with the skill purpose, but executing repository scripts can modify files or run untrusted code in a compromised repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly listed in the skill instructions. The context supports legitimate system maintenance, but the risk remains because repository-local scripts execute with the agent session permissions.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography findings point to ordinary prose, directory names, or Markdown file references. No hash algorithm, encryption operation, or cryptographic decision is present at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited line found no cryptographic code or algorithm names. The signals appear to be substring matches inside non-code Markdown text.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Detections Are Benign Audit Instructions
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance findings refer to bounded repository inspection, such as checking logs and citations. No instruction collects host secrets, network data, credentials, or operating system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe limiting shell output, checking citations, and reading a project communication log. These actions are scoped to project quality control rather than host reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes explicit instructions to run project-local commands. Reviewers should treat this as a controlled external-command capability rather than as Ruby backtick execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are present in Markdown instructions and are semantically intended to be run. The evidence does not show obfuscation, network access, or credential handling.

content.value_statement: Complex evidence workflows can lose context, citations, and ownership across multiple agents. This skill creates a plan-first coordination layer for tests, delegation, audits, and quality control.
actual_capabilities: Requires a written plan before major workflow actions.
Routes work to named Pukaist agent roles by task type.
Directs agents to run repository integrity and health checks.
Defines checks for logs, queues, citations, and output quality.
Applies a neutral clerk standard for evidence reporting.
limitations: Depends on repository files and scripts that may not exist elsewhere.
Can only coordinate agents when the host tool supports delegation.
Does not perform OCR, analysis, or legal synthesis by itself.
Requires human approval before delegated execution.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Evidence Intake
use_cases.description: Create an approved sequence for ingestion, OCR, review, and logging before any agent changes files.

use_cases.title: Audit a Review Pipeline
use_cases.description: Check queues, logs, citations, and refined reports for consistency before marking work complete.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Specialist Agents
use_cases.description: Assign work to Gatekeeper, Scribe, Analyst, Archivist, Historian, or Barrister roles based on the task.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Work Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use manager-planner to draft a step-by-step plan for this evidence workflow. Do not execute the plan until I approve it.

prompt_templates.title: Check System Health
prompt_templates.prompt: Use manager-planner to check whether the evidence workflow is healthy. Summarize required tests, log checks, and queue checks before running commands.

prompt_templates.title: Delegate Agent Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use manager-planner to assign this request to the correct Pukaist agent roles. Include acceptance criteria for each delegated task.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Final Outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use manager-planner to perform a final quality audit across refined reports, citations, queue state, and communication logs. Report blockers first.

output_examples.input: Plan the intake of three new evidence files.
output_examples.output: A numbered plan that assigns intake to Gatekeeper, OCR checks to Scribe, review to Analyst, and final validation to Manager.

output_examples.input: Check whether the system is working as intended.
output_examples.output: A concise audit summary covering tests, health checks, orphan files, stalled queues, citation integrity, and open blockers.

output_examples.input: Approve completed analyst work for one theme.
output_examples.output: A verification checklist that samples outputs, confirms citations, checks neutral language, and identifies the final approval step.

best_practices: Ask for a plan before allowing agents to modify files.
Run integrity checks before major operations or approvals.
Verify citations and queue status before marking work complete.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to bypass user approval for implementation.
Running cleanup or reconciliation commands without reviewing scope.
Treating agent summaries as evidence without checking citations.

faq.question: What does this skill coordinate?
faq.answer: It coordinates Pukaist evidence-review roles, planning, tests, delegation, and quality checks.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: It instructs agents to run repository-local commands for tests, health checks, cleanup, and reconciliation.

faq.question: Is it suitable outside the original repository?
faq.answer: Only with adaptation. Many paths and agent names are specific to the original workflow.

faq.question: Does it replace human approval?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires plan approval before delegated execution.

faq.question: Can it perform legal analysis?
faq.answer: No. It coordinates a Barrister role but does not provide legal advice by itself.

faq.question: What security review is recommended?
faq.answer: Review repository scripts before execution, especially cleanup and reconciliation utilities.
